Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 15:31 ET
P/C divergence signals dispersion trade with -$12.3B negative gamma amplifying moves
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts heavy (P/C 2.51) vs single-name calls (0.86) — classic dispersion setup
  • 2. Negative $12.3B SPY gamma amplifies all moves with flip at $666.32
  • 3. Smart money reversals: Millennium added AMZN 398% Q4 after Q3 reduction
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge with gamma risk

P/C ratios reveal a stark divergence that defines current positioning: index puts dominate with P/C at 2.51 while single-name calls prevail at 0.86, creating a textbook dispersion trade setup amid implied correlation at 0.40. This institutional hedge pattern — portfolio protection via index puts while maintaining single-name upside exposure — confirms the stock-specific regime even as VIX sits at 26.6 with F&G at extreme fear (15/100). The 434K+ SPY put blocks in Mar 24 strikes underscore the systematic nature of this positioning. Negative gamma at -$12.3B on SPY creates mechanical amplification risk into Core PCE (3 days out), with the gamma flip at $666.32 sitting $11 above current spot of $655.11. This narrow buffer means any catalyst-driven move above $666 triggers mechanical dealer buying that accelerates momentum independent of fundamentals. ARM exemplifies the cross-currents: +234% call flow despite technical death cross, suggesting earnings positioning overrides chart patterns. Energy emerges as the cleanest bullish setup with XLE showing golden cross technicals (score 89.5), RSI at overbought 77, yet 5K Jun 65C blocks suggest further upside targeting. The sector's technical strength contrasts sharply with mega-cap tech deterioration where MSFT and META show death crosses with bearish scores below 20. Millennium's 398% Q4 AMZN reversal after Q3 reduction signals smart money repositioning that preceded current call flows.

DESKUS Today's Top News9:29 AM ET
________________________________ Synopsis (last modified 08:00 ET on 24-Mar-26) S&P futures lower: * S&P futures down 0.1% in Tuesday morning trading after US equities rallied to start the week, reversing overnight losses. All 11 S&P 500 sectors finished higher with more than 80% of the index up for the day. Small caps, travel/tourism, homebuilders, metals, machinery, rare earths and quantum computing some of the notable outperformers. Memory one of the higher-profile laggards. Treasuries weaker
________________________________ Synopsis (last modified 08:00 ET on 24-Mar-26) S&P futures lower: * S&P futures down 0.1% in Tuesday morning trading after US equities rallied to start the week, reversing overnight losses. All 11 S&P 500 sectors finished higher with more than 80% of the index up for the day. Small caps, travel/tourism, homebuilders, metals, machinery, rare earths and quantum computing some of the notable outperformers. Memory one of the higher-profile laggards. Treasuries weaker with yields up 3-4 bp. Dollar index up 0.3%. Gold little changed. Silver up 1.2%. Bitcoin futures up 0.3%. WTI crude up 3.2%. Brent up 2.7%. * Quieter from a headline perspective. Iran continues to largely deny ceasefire negotiations with the US but not necessarily surprising and the stream of backchannel talk headlines suggest something is going on. However, Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed, a report yesterday said there are at least a dozen Iranian mines in the Strait, Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly considering joining war against Iran, US still sending thousands more troops to the region and Israel and Iran have fired more missiles today. Outside of geopolitics, private credit worries back in the headlines with APO sticking to its 5% withdrawal cap on its flagship fund despite receiving redemption requests for more than double that amount. Also some focus on stagflation signals from flash Eurozone PMIs for March. * Final Q4 nonfarm productivity and unit labor costs and flash PMIs for March on the US economic calendar this morning. PMIs expected to get even more attention than usual given they represent the first batch of data reflecting the Iran conflict. Treasury to sell $69B of 2-year notes. In addition, Governor Barr scheduled to speak this evening. February import/export price data out on Wednesday, initial claims the lone release for Thursday morning and final University of Michigan consumer sentiment (and inflation expectations) for March caps off the week on Friday. * APO said investors looked to pull 11.2% (~$1.6B) from its flagship private credit fund but it will stick to its 5% threshold. FSK cut to junk at Moody's. JEF higher on FT report Japan's SFMG considering takeover (though nothing imminent). GILD confirmed a deal to acquire autoimmune disease-focused biotech Ouro Medicines for $1,675M in upfront cash consideration and up to $500M in contingent milestone payments. EL confirmed reports that surfaced just before the close on Monday that it is in talks to acquire Spain's Puig. NTGR up big on Reuters report FCC banning the import of all new foreign-made consumer routers. BABA launched new chip for agentic IA and inference computing. SK Hynix considering ~$10B US listing. Top Stories (last modified 07:24 ET on 24-Mar-26) Morning Headlines: * Iran conflict: * Trump's reprieve offer came after US and Gulf allies warned about dangers of striking Iran's energy sites (link <https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/other/the-back-channel-diplomacy-behind-trump-s-u-turn-on-iran/ar-AA1ZfXwS> , missiles into Israel, continues to dismiss Trump's negotiation claims (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/iran-sends-waves-missiles-into-israel-dismisses-trumps-talk-negotiations-fake-2026-03-24>, Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/iran-strikes-persist-even-as-trump-claims-talks-to-end-conflict> , Times of Israel to energy sites (Semafor<https://www.semafor.com/article/03/23/2026/us-to-keep-attacking-iran-as-israel-stays-on-sidelines-of-talks>) * Gulf states nearing decision to join fight against Iran following relentless missile and drone attacks (link<https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/gulf-states-edge-toward-joining-fight-against-iran/ar-AA1ZfGxa>, Bloomberg weighing risks (Jerusalem Post <https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/iran-news/article-890971> ) * US intelligence reports indicate over a dozen Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz (CBS News hope for in-person de-escalation talks with Iran (London Times <https://www.thetimes.com/us/news-today/article/witkoff-travels-to-pakistan-in-hope-of-in-person-talks-with-iran-pn732tdb5> , NBC News credit rating cut to junk at Moody's on concerns about asset quality better amid Iran war, but risks of severe declines remain if conflict continues (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/iran-fallout-us-shares-hold-up-better-than-global-rivals-now-2026-03-24> ) * (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/ecb-must-be-vigilant-in-face-of-stagflation-risks-vujcic-says>) * RBNZ Governor Breman signals rate hike if inflation stays higher for Department to suspend Nvidia's AI chip export licenses to China, Southeast Asia (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/556e534d-bbc5-46e0-8965-ec3a13a8871a?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * US congressional advisory body warns China's lower cost, open-source AI is giving it competitive advantage Europe warns EU faces higher tariffs if parliament doesn't approve trade deal (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-23/us-envoy-warns-eu-can-expect-higher-tariffs-if-trade-deal-dies>, FT <https://www.ft.com/content/6bf153e4-11af-44d5-9d1c-48b5c7ad26ef?accessToken=zwAAAZ22FhyGkc9r8VPkEa9E1dOdHEi1x60m7wE.MEQCIGxsQoKLRN2PuwDwBjG3oNoXM345IuHdRF5UeuBk0ndkAiBVJqr4dPWIFpTspxWMChSEnhRKF-Q-H7AKJbEDvzVRSw&segmentId=7d4bcc2e-e664-92ba-62e3-5590579f1902&syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Washington: * Senate Republicans, after meeting Trump, claim to have a solution for DHS shutdown(Politico <https://www.politico.com/news/2026/03/23/dhs-shutdown-talks-breakthrough-00841195> , Axios<https://www.axios.com/2026/03/23/dhs-funding-house-senate-budget-talks-reconciliation>, link <https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/politics/senators-close-in-on-deal-to-end-dhs-shutdown-fund-tsa/ar-AA1ZeECu> ) * Supreme Court appears poised to limit counting of mail-in ballots arriving after Election Day (Washington Post<https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/03/23/supreme-court-mail-in-ballots-mississippi>) Tuesday morning check-in: * Quieter from a headline perspective. Iran continues to largely deny ceasefire negotiations with the US but not necessarily surprising and the stream of backchannel talk headlines over the last 24 hours or so suggest something is going on. * However, Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed, report yesterday said there are at least a dozen Iranian mines in the Strait, Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly considering joining war against Iran, US still sending thousands more troops to the region and Israel and Iran have fired more missiles today. * Outside of geopolitics, private credit worries back in the headlines with Apollo sticking to its 5% withdrawal cap on its flagship fund despite receiving redemption requests for more than double that amount. In addition, FS KKR credit rating cut to junk at Moody's on concerns about asset quality. * Also some focus on stagflation signals from flash Eurozone PMIs for March. S&P Global flash composite PMI fell to 50.5 from 51.9 in prior month, below 51.0 consensus and weakest reading since last May. Input cost inflation accelerated to fastest pace in over three years. * Lot of uncertainty about what's next. Monday move flagged as more about short covering/hedge lifting (particularly at the ETF level) than any reset in risk appetite. At the same time, magnitude and duration of the drawdown continue to fit with historical geopolitical shock pattern (which has been a dip buying opportunity). Back-channel diplomacy may be taking place, but Iran war remains hot (and could pull in other regional powers): * Market still focused on trying to flesh out Trump's Monday claim of diplomacy with new Iranian leadership. No specifics from the White House and nothing further (yet) on Truth Social. Iranian sources have denied any direct talks and administration has said contacts at an early stage (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/23/us/politics/trump-iran-gas-oil-strait.html>). However, a lot of references to possible indirect/back-channel contacts, with Omani, Egyptian, and Pakistani officials mentioned (CBS News<https://www.cbsnews.com/news/iran-us-message-mediators-trump-iran-war-possible-deal>, link<https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/the-back-channel-diplomacy-behind-trump-s-u-turn-on-iran/ar-AA1ZfXwS?ocid=weather-verthp-feeds>, AP<https://apnews.com/live/iran-war-israel-trump-03-24-2026#0000019d-1e91-d5b1-a19f-9effb3bf0000>). Note Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-23/trump-s-market-driven-reversal-buys-time-but-skepticism-abounds?srnd=homepage-americas> reported yesterday's de-escalatory tone came after regional allies warned Trump the war was becoming a disaster. * Meanwhile, conflict continues across the region, with Iran launching multiple strikes at Israel, US Mideast bases, and other regional powers; Israel has continued pressing in Lebanon (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/iran-strikes-persist-even-as-trump-claims-talks-to-end-conflict>). US officials note there are now at least a dozen underwater mines in the Strait of Hormuz (CBS News<https://www.cbsnews.com/news/strait-of-hormuz-mines-iran-talks-officials/>). And a lot of focus on a media report<https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/gulf-states-edge-toward-joining-fight-against-iran/ar-AA1ZfGxa?cvid=7f2ca8be935149aeee24a11a71a333dc&ocid=BingNewsVerp> Saudi Arabia may be near entering the war while the UAE is beginning to crack down on Iran-owned assets and debating military involvement. * Ultimately, a fair bit of skepticism about Trump's diplomatic claims, with observers seeing a real possibility of Trump executing postponed strikes on Iranian electrical infrastructure by the end of the week. And note a Marine Expeditionary Unit (2.5K troops) is en route to the region, while senior military officials also consider deployment of elements of the 82nd Airborne (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/23/us/politics/us-airborne-troops-iran.html>). * Possibility of diplomacy delivered a bit of relief for energy prices yesterday with WTI dropping more than 10%, though crude giving some of that back today. Among today's energy headlines, Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/fees-to-ship-saudi-crude-from-yanbu-ease-as-more-tankers-arrive> reports more tankers arriving at Saudi Arabia's Red Sea port of Yanbu to pick up diverted flows. Indian buyers uncertain about buying Iranian cargoes despite US waivers (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/us-approved-iranian-barrels-find-a-cautious-reception-in-india>). And White House says it has not received pushback from US producers about ramping up domestic production (Politico<https://www.politico.com/news/2026/03/23/iran-oil-agen-production-energy-00841108>). Private credit continues to make headlines: * Apollo said that while investors looked to pull ~$1.6B from its Apollo Debt Solutions BDC, representing 11.2% of its net assets, it stuck to its 5% withdrawal cap. Highlighted need to balance interest of investors seeking liquidity with those who choose to stay invested. * FT<https://www.ft.com/content/d315cb1c-1e1e-479c-a6d4-b3a817fead3e?syn-25a6b1a6=1> said that based on its estimates, investors have submitted redemption requests worth $11.7B across more than half a dozen funds it tracks. Added that thus far, only 66% of withdrawals have been met, totaling $7.8B. * While retail redemptions and software exposure (AI disruption terminal value debate) have grabbed most of the attention, credit quality also in play. FS KKR credit rating cut to junk at Moody's. Flagged continued asset quality challenges. * Sell-side research continues to paint a much less dire scenario than the financial press. Goldman Sachs said private credit stress is unlikely to generate large macroeconomic [...truncated to 2000 words]
OVI Scanner Context
MARKET CONTEXT (recent headlines): - [ft_markets] GB Energy chair calls for more North Sea oil production - [marketwatch] Oil prices rally as Pentagon reportedly plans to deploy 3,000 troops to the Middle East - [ft_markets] Oil rises back above $100 as Iran launches new strikes

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
